Cape Angler
  • Knots
  • Spots
  • Reading

Cape Town · Western Cape

Tie stronger knots.
Fish better spots.

An immersive reference for recreational anglers on the Cape. The essential knots, the spots worth driving to, and the reading worth doing — without the clutter.

Start with knotsExplore spots

01 — Essentials

Knots that cover almost every day on the water.

Tap a knot to see the step-by-step plus an animated and a real-life demo video. Learn these cold and you’ll lose fewer fish, faster.

02 — On the water

Spots worth the drive.

A curated map of 20 shore, rock, harbour and estuary marks plus 6 freshwater dams and rivers around the Cape. Each card lists the best spots within that area, and you can open a satellite close-up before you drive.

  • Kalk Bay Harbour

    Harbour & pier

    Working fishing harbour on the False Bay coast. Pier and harbour wall access, good for hottentot, galjoen and the occasional yellowtail. Family-friendly and easy shore access.

    Best spots within

    • •Outer harbour wall on the eastern side — best at high tide for hottentot and panga.
    • •Tip of the main jetty — light tackle for steentjies; live mussel works.
    • •The corner where the slipway meets the inner wall — kob in late autumn evenings.
    Skill level:
    Beginner-friendly
    Permit:
    A recreational saltwater fishing permit (post office) is required.
    Google MapsDirections

  • Miller's Point

    Rock & surf

    Classic Cape rock-angling venue south of Simon's Town. Deep water close in — targets include kob, yellowtail and geelbek. Watch the swell; not a spot for novices on a pushing tide.

    Best spots within

    • •The point itself, accessed from the parking area — yellowtail when the south-easter blows.
    • •Slipway gully (north side of the parking) — sheltered option for hottentot in heavy swell.
    • •Castle Rocks ledge, 200 m south — deep water for kob; only on a low and dropping swell.
    Skill level:
    Experienced anglers
    Permit:
    Recreational saltwater permit required. Falls within the Table Mountain National Park MPA — check current zoning.
    Google MapsDirections

  • Simon's Town Jetty

    Jetty

    Sheltered jetty fishing inside Simon's Town naval harbour area. Hottentot, steentjies and panga from the wall. Good winter option when the rocks are blown out.

    Best spots within

    • •Long Beach side of the jetty (south-facing) — most consistent for hottentot.
    • •The corner where the wooden walkway meets the wall — deeper water at high tide.
    • •Inside the small craft mooring area — light sabikis for maasbanker bait.
    Skill level:
    Beginner-friendly
    Permit:
    Recreational saltwater permit required.
    Google MapsDirections

  • Strand Beach

    Surf

    Long sandy surf beach on the eastern side of False Bay. Kob, elf (shad) and white steenbras are the main targets. Best fished early morning or late afternoon on a pushing tide.

    Best spots within

    • •Macassar dunes end (eastern Strand) — quieter, deep gully runs hard against the beach for kob.
    • •In front of the lifesaver club — easy parking, good for shad on a pushing tide at dawn.
    • •Lourens River mouth (western end) — leervis and grunter on a strong incoming tide.
    Skill level:
    Beginner-friendly
    Permit:
    Recreational saltwater permit required. Observe seasonal closures for shad (elf).
    Google MapsDirections

03 — Useful reading

Know before you cast.

Background reading on permits, species, tides and catch-and-release — the bits that turn a day out into a catch.

  • Department of Forestry, Fisheries & the Environment

    Recreational Fishing Permits — South Africa

    What permits you need to fish recreationally in South Africa, where to get them, and the species limits that apply.

    6 min readdffe.gov.za

  • South African Association for Marine Biological Research

    Common Shore Species of the Western Cape

    Identification guide and size-limit reference for the fish you're most likely to land from Cape shorelines.

    10 min readsaambr.org.za

  • SA Navy Hydrographic Office

    Reading Tides — When to Fish the Cape Coast

    Official tide tables for Cape Town and surrounding ports, plus guidance on which phase of the tide fishes best for common species.

    5 min readsanho.co.za

Cape Angler

A reference site for recreational anglers on the Cape. No accounts. No tracking pixels beyond what Vercel ships.

Fish ethically, keep what you’ll eat.

© 2026 Cape Angler · Cape Town, South Africa.
